nodes.label: Issue: Multiprocessing ProcessPoolExecutor crashes on Windows
nodes.norm_label: issue: multiprocessing processpoolexecutor crashes on windows

nodes.label: Graphify Knowledge Graph Rules
nodes.rationale: Specifies how codebase/architecture questions query the graph, skip conditions, and auto-updating practices.

nodes.label: Windows Multiprocessing Fix
nodes.rationale: Windows lacks automatic fork mechanism, causing process pool crashes during script execution unless run single-threaded.

nodes.label: FSD Isolation Principle Details
nodes.rationale: Logic or UI needed by multiple features must be refactored into the global shared layer to keep features strictly decoupled.

nodes.label: FSD Import Rules Details
nodes.rationale: Enforces strict import boundaries where feature modules can only consume shared elements, other parts of the same feature, but never elements of another feature.

nodes.label: Contributing FSD Guidelines
nodes.rationale: Reiterates the FSD isolation principle and folder structures for contributing developers.

nodes.label: Code Verification & Style Gates
nodes.rationale: Linting, type checking, formatting, and validation scripts are run automatically or manually to enforce code style guidelines before commits.

nodes.label: Pull Request Quality Checklist
nodes.rationale: Ensures PRs pass validation, conform to FSD structure without cross-feature imports, have database migrations, and build successfully.

nodes.label: Strict Payment Methods
nodes.rationale: To prevent scope creep and support efficient storefront operations, operations are strictly restricted to Cash and GCash. Do not integrate Credit Cards, Maya, or other digital wallets without formal scrum review.

nodes.label: No Delivery Architecture
nodes.rationale: Operations focus purely on walk-in and in-store pickup. Do not build shipping modules, fleet tracking, customer address managers, or dispatch pipelines.

nodes.label: Role-Based Security
nodes.rationale: Staff and cashiers must be branch-locked; they must never query or mutate data belonging to other branches. Only Admins possess cross-branch query privileges.
